headline: Tech Bulls Unite as AI Models Bet on QQQ and NVDA Rally
summary: All five AI models showed remarkable consensus on tech sector strength, with unanimous bullish calls on QQQ (ranging from $455 to $705 targets) and near-unanimous agreement on NVDA's upside. The models split on TSLA direction, with Claude and Grok bearish while Gemini and GPT-4o remained optimistic, highlighting divergent views on the EV maker's prospects amid mixed Q1 results.

consensus_picks.note: Four models aligned bullish on QQQ with confidence levels between 0.6-0.78, citing tech sector strength and AMD earnings spillover.
All four models predicting SPY went bullish with moderate confidence (0.55-0.7), expecting continued momentum from the 3.1% five-day gain.
Three models agreed on NVDA upside with Grok most confident at 0.7, all citing AI chip demand and AMD earnings validation.
Sharp division with Claude and Grok bearish citing Q1 delivery miss, while Gemini and GPT-4o bullish on product announcement hopes.

best_call.summary: Claude showed highest conviction (0.78) on QQQ reaching $705, backed by specific pre-market data and AMD earnings catalyst.

worst_call.summary: Perplexity's weak 0.45 confidence on TSLA downside reflected uncertainty rather than conviction in either direction.
